title: Workflow Automation
description: Test your knowledge of automating tasks with AI-driven workflows

questions.question: What is 'tool use' (function calling) in an AI workflow?
options: The model letting an application run predefined functions on its behalf
A way to physically build hardware tools
Manually copying model output into a spreadsheet
Restarting the model between steps
questions.explanation: Tool use lets the model request that the surrounding application call defined functions — search, calculators, APIs — and feed the results back into the conversation.

questions.question: Why is a 'human-in-the-loop' checkpoint valuable in an automated workflow?
options: It lets a person review or approve high-stakes actions before they execute
It makes the workflow run faster
It removes the need for any testing
It hides errors from the user
questions.explanation: A human checkpoint catches mistakes and gives oversight on consequential or irreversible actions before the automation commits to them.

questions.question: What is 'orchestration' in a multi-step AI workflow?
options: Coordinating the order, inputs and outputs of multiple steps or agents
Playing background music while the model runs
Compressing the model to a smaller size
Encrypting the API key
questions.explanation: Orchestration is the control layer that decides which steps run, in what order, and how each step's output feeds the next.

questions.question: Why should automated workflows handle model errors and retries explicitly?
options: Model calls can fail or return malformed output, and unhandled failures break the pipeline
Retries make the model smarter over time
It is only needed for image models
Errors never happen in production
questions.explanation: Network issues, rate limits and malformed responses are normal; explicit error handling and retries keep the overall workflow reliable.

questions.question: What is 'idempotency' and why does it matter for automation?
options: Running the same step twice produces the same result without duplicate side effects
The model forgets previous steps
Every step must run exactly once or the system crashes
It is a way to measure token cost
questions.explanation: An idempotent step can be safely retried because repeating it doesn't create duplicate records or effects — essential when a workflow may re-run after a failure.
